key_highlights: US inflation surged to 3.8% in April, highest in nearly three years, lifting rate-hike odds and pressuring gold and bonds.
Iran war developments continue to strain oil flows and aluminum supply, with oil steady after three-day gain.
AI and data center spending remain strong, driving gains in Fabrinet, Comfort Systems USA, and Cerebras IPO.
China dominates offshore wind and EV markets, but US-China tensions persist with Trump facing emboldened Xi.
India hikes gold import tariffs to protect economy, while S&P affirms its strength despite foreign outflows.
Bitcoin opens at its strongest in months, while crypto regulation battles heat up with digital euro and Tether.
Consumer staples and defensive sectors face headwinds from inflation and tariff fears, but some stocks like Nike see insider buying.
Geopolitical risks (Iran, US-China, Australia budget) and macro uncertainty dominate the outlook.

sectors.trend: Strong upward momentum driven by AI optimism, data center spending, and IPO activity (Cerebras raising $4.8B). Fabrinet, Comfort Systems USA, and Micron are beneficiaries.
sectors.implications: Investors should overweight AI infrastructure plays, but watch for valuation risks and potential regulatory scrutiny on AI models (e.g., OpenAI vs. Musk).

sectors.trend: Oil steadies after three-day gain as Iran war strains flows. Aluminum shortfall 'yet to be fully experienced' per Timna Tanners. Soybean meal prices rise on inflation fears.
sectors.implications: Energy and commodity stocks may benefit from supply disruptions, but rate-hike fears and potential peace talks (Trump rejects Iran peace plan) add volatility. Consider hedging with energy ETFs.

sectors.trend: Gold and silver decline as US inflation data lifts rate-hike odds and Trump rejects Iran peace plan. India hikes gold import tariffs.
sectors.implications: Short-term headwinds from higher rates, but long-term inflation and geopolitical risks support a strategic allocation. Wait for dip-buying opportunities.

sectors.trend: Grocery prices jumped most in nearly four years. Wendy's future hinges on chicken sandwich. Nike CEO buys $2M worth of shares at low point. DoorDash gains market share.
sectors.implications: Inflation pressures squeeze margins, but selective plays like DoorDash (delivery) and Nike (insider confidence) may outperform. Avoid overvalued staples ETFs.

sectors.trend: Presentations at BofA Healthcare Conference (Butterfly Network, Intellia, Astrana Health). Isomorphic Labs secures $2.1B for AI drug design. Enliven Therapeutics positive Phase 1 data.
sectors.implications: AI in drug discovery is a growing theme. Intellia (gene editing) and Isomorphic (AI) are high-risk/high-reward. Monitor conference takeaways for near-term catalysts.

sectors.trend: BofA drops blunt warning about Fed rate cuts. Goldman sees dollar strength on energy shock. Asia stocks erase losses, bonds fall on US inflation. India's economic strength defies outflows.
sectors.implications: Banks may benefit from higher rates, but recession fears linger. Dollar strength pressures EM. Favor US large-cap banks with strong capital positions.

sectors.trend: NYC buyers holding off (Brown Harris Stevens CEO). Australia tackles property tax breaks. Trump Tower plan for Gold Coast scrapped. Geothermal developer Fervo raises $1.89B IPO.
sectors.implications: Real estate faces headwinds from high rates and inflation. Infrastructure (data centers, renewable energy) offers growth. Fervo IPO signals investor appetite for green energy.

sectors.trend: Chinese EVs dominate globally except US. Audi Q9 raises interior bar. GM lays off IT workers to hire AI talent. Trump may support suspending federal gas tax.
sectors.implications: EV adoption continues but geopolitical barriers remain. GM's AI pivot is positive. Gas tax suspension could temporarily boost auto demand but hurt EV incentives.

sectors.trend: EchoStar reports mixed Q1 with subscriber losses. Fox shares rise on ad strength. Netflix sued by Texas AG for alleged surveillance. Trump Mobile changes terms after $100 deposits.
sectors.implications: Cord-cutting pressures legacy telecom. Fox benefits from ad market recovery. Netflix legal risk is a headwind. Avoid speculative Trump-related ventures.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate debt and inflation-protected securities (TIPS). Consider short-term treasuries or money market funds.
Increase exposure to energy stocks (XLE) and commodity ETFs (DBC). Hedge with oil futures or options. Monitor peace talks and supply data.
Diversify supply chains; invest in domestic manufacturing and defense stocks. Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors (semiconductors, EVs).
Focus on defensive consumer staples with pricing power (e.g., Costco, Procter & Gamble). Avoid highly leveraged retailers. Monitor grocery price trends.
Reduce exposure to EM bonds and currencies. Favor India (strong fundamentals) over Mexico (negative outlook) and Indonesia (rupiah pressure).
Avoid stocks with high regulatory exposure (Netflix, Trump Mobile). Monitor digital euro developments and crypto regulation. Favor compliant AI companies.
Diversify geographically. UK gilts under pressure from political instability. Australia budget may hit consumer stocks. NZ isolation no longer a security guarantee.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure plays (Fabrinet, Comfort Systems USA, Micron) on pullbacks.
opportunities.rationale: Strong data center spending and AI adoption drive secular growth. Cerebras IPO success confirms investor appetite.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate gold on dips as a hedge against inflation and geopolitical risk.
opportunities.rationale: Inflation at 3.8% and Iran war support gold long-term, despite short-term rate-hike pressure. India tariff hike may reduce demand but supply constraints persist.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy stocks (XLE) to benefit from Iran supply disruption.
opportunities.rationale: Oil steadies after gains; aluminum shortfall not yet priced. Energy sector offers both income and upside from supply constraints.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider India-focused ETFs for resilience amid EM weakness.
opportunities.rationale: S&P affirms India's economic strength despite foreign outflows. Strong domestic demand and reform momentum.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy DoorDash (DASH) on market share gains and delivery resilience.
opportunities.rationale: Results reinforced market share gains; consumer shift to delivery persists despite inflation.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore AI healthcare plays (Intellia, Isomorphic Labs) for long-term growth.
opportunities.rationale: AI drug design is transformative. Isomorphic's $2.1B funding and positive trial data support the thesis.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce bond duration; shift to short-term treasuries or TIPS.
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation surge and rate-hike odds make long-duration bonds risky. TIPS provide inflation protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge portfolio with put options on S&P 500 or buy VIX calls.
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risks (Iran, US-China) and macro uncertainty warrant tail-risk hedging. Market sentiment is neutral but fragile.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to EM bonds and currencies; favor USD cash or US money market funds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar strength (Goldman sees energy shock keeping rates high) pressures EM. Mexico outlook negative, rupiah falling.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid consumer discretionary stocks with weak pricing power.
defensive_moves.rationale: Grocery prices surge, consumer spending under pressure. Wendy's and other fast-food chains face margin compression.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Sell or avoid Trump-related speculative assets (Trump Mobile, Trump Media).
defensive_moves.rationale: Trump Mobile changes terms after deposits; high regulatory and reputational risk. No clear path to profitability.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile as inflation data (3.8%) and Iran war developments dominate. The Fed is on hold with rate-hike odds rising, pressuring bonds and gold. AI and energy sectors may outperform, while consumer and EM face headwinds. S&P 500 and Nasdaq are at record highs but vulnerable to profit-taking.
market_outlook.long_term: Inflation persistence and geopolitical fragmentation (US-China, Iran) will keep risk premiums elevated. AI and data center spending will drive structural growth in tech. Energy and commodity stocks benefit from supply constraints. Dollar strength persists, pressuring EM. India and select Asian markets offer relative resilience. Crypto may see increased institutional adoption despite regulatory battles.
key_catalysts: US CPI and PCE data releases in coming weeks
Fed meeting minutes and rate decision (June)
Cerebras IPO pricing and aftermarket performance
Q1 earnings season results (especially tech and consumer)
India budget and tariff policy changes
Digital euro and crypto regulation developments
Australia federal budget impact on consumer stocks
watch_list: WTI crude oil price (above $90/bbl risks recession)
China offshore wind and EV export data
India rupee and gold import data
AI chip demand indicators (Nvidia, AMD earnings)
